akshaya-tritiya-2026.fullDescription: Akshaya Tritiya is one of the most auspicious days in the Hindu calendar. The word 'Akshaya' means 'eternal' or 'that which never diminishes'. It is believed that any act of charity (Daan) performed on this day yields never-ending blessings for the donor. At Karamsthali NGO, we celebrate this day by inviting our supporters to contribute towards 'Anna Daan' (food) and 'Gau Sewa' (cow care). This is the perfect time to make a lasting impact on the lives of those who struggle for basic necessities. Your contributions on this powerful day help us maintain our feeding drives and medical care for abandoned animals throughout the year.
highlights: Jwara Daan: Providing fresh green fodder to local Gaushalas
Anna Daan: Large-scale food distribution for homeless families
Jal Daan: Placing earthen water pots for birds and animals
Vastra Daan: Distributing summer clothing to underprivileged children
Special prayers and Bhakt Sewa at our community centers

agenda.activity: Gau Sewa & Fodder Distribution at Balicha Gaushala
Anna Daan: Serving wholesome meals near Railway Station
Water Stall (Pyau) Setup and Thirsty Traveler Service

sita-navami-2026.fullDescription: Sita Navami celebrates the appearance of Devi Sita, the consort of Lord Ram and a manifestation of Goddess Lakshmi. Her life is a testament to resilience, sacrifice, and boundless compassion. On this day, we focus our energy on supporting the most vulnerable women and children in our community. Donating food and clothes on Sita Navami is considered highly meritorious. Karamsthali NGO organizes dedicated drives to ensure that the spirit of 'Mata Sita'—the provider and nurturer—reaches every home that faces hardship. Join us in bringing peace and prosperity to families through your generous contributions.
highlights: Poshahar Vitran: Distributing nutritious kits to pregnant women
Kanya Pujan: Special meals and educational support for young girls
Vastra Daan: Donating sarees and suits to widow homes
Sita-Ram Bhog: Preparing and distributing prasad to thousands
Awareness sessions on women's rights and health

agenda.activity: Nutritional Kit Distribution at Rural Health Centers
Clothing Distribution Drive for Slum Dwellers

vaishakha-purnima-2026.fullDescription: The Purnima of the Vaishakha month, also known as Buddha Purnima, is an exceptionally sacred day for charity. It marks the birth, enlightenment, and nirvana of Lord Buddha, who taught the world that the greatest religion is compassion. On this day, we prioritize 'Jiva Daya' (kindness to all living beings). The summer heat is at its peak, making it vital to provide cooling relief to those working on the streets. Your Daan of water, refreshing fruits, and shelter tools can provide immediate relief to laborers, street vendors, and the elderly who face the harsh sun every day.
highlights: Phal Daan: Distributing muskmelons, watermelons, and bananas
Sheetal Jal: Serving cold water and lemonade from mobile vans
Umbrella Distribution: Providing shade to street vendors
Animal Water Points: Installing concrete troughs for strays
Health monitoring for heat-stroke prevention

agenda.activity: Mobile Water Distribution in Industrial Areas
Installing Animal Water Points across the city

shani-jayanti-2026.fullDescription: Shani Jayanti is the birth anniversary of Lord Shani (Saturn), the god of justice and karma. It is widely believed that serving the poor and the marginalized on this day is the most effective way to appease Lord Shani and reduce the burden of one's misdeeds. Shani Dev is particularly associated with those who labor under difficult conditions. At Karamsthali, we observe this day with 'Shramik Seva'—serving our laborer brothers and sisters. By donating footwear, clothing, and essential food items, you not only help someone in need but also align yourself with the principles of justice and selfless service.
highlights: Paduka Daan: Distributing slippers and footwear to barefoot children
Vastra Daan: Providing black umbrellas and clothing (Shani Dev's favorites)
Tail Daan: Collecting and distributing cooking oil to BPL families
Til & Anna: Distributing black sesame and nutritional grains
Inaugurating health camps for construction laborers

agenda.activity: Footwear Distribution Drive at Construction Sites
Essential Oil & Grain Distribution for the Needy
Blanket and Clothing Distribution for the Homeless

nirjala-ekadashi-2026.fullDescription: Nirjala Ekadashi is the most significant and powerful of all 24 Ekadashis. 'Nirjala' means without water, as the traditional fast on this day is observed without consuming even a drop of water. Because of its severity, it provides the benefits of all other Ekadashis combined. For this reason, 'Jal Daan' (water donation) on this day is considered the highest form of merit. As the peak of summer approaches, the thirsty cry out for relief. Karamsthali NGO launches its largest annual water initiative on Nirjala Ekadashi, setting up drinking stations and distributing cooling refreshments to thousands.
highlights: Amrut Pyau: Establishing 50+ water stalls across Udaipur
Matka Daan: Distributing earthen pots to slum areas for natural cooling
Sharbat Vitran: Serving rose and khus coolers to commuters
Jal Sewa: Distributing bottled water to traffic police and laborers
Special prayers for rain and environmental balance

agenda.activity: City-wide Sharbat and Cold Water Distribution
Volunteer Appreciation and Water Conservation Talk
